title: Tool Choice, Evidence, Actions, and Approval Boundaries · Prompt Forensics
summary: Design a tool-using investigation that reads evidence before changing state. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a safe tool-use decision protocol and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Write tool descriptions and schemas that make capability, inputs, outputs, and side effects unambiguous.
Sequence read-only discovery before mutations unless the user explicitly requests an immediate safe action.
Base the next decision on actual tool results and distinguish inference from observation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Tool Failure Recovery evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Tool Failure Recovery assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Handle timeouts, partial results, retries, and alternate evidence without looping blindly. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt tools actions. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Tool Failure Recovery, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a safe tool-use decision protocol.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Repeating the identical failing tool call is a robust recovery strategy. This fails the Tool Failure Recovery contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Anthropic Agent Evaluation Guidance (2026) — Controls multi-turn and tool-using evaluation design for agentic prompts.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle timeouts, partial results, retries, and alternate evidence without looping blindly.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Repeating the identical failing tool call is a robust recovery strategy.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-tool-failure-recovery-hsphst" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Tool Failure Recovery into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-tool-failure-recovery-hsphst
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Tool Failure Recovery applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Handle timeouts, partial results, retries, and alternate evidence without looping blindly.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
